summary.title: Data Cloud Tenant Isolation and Governance Plane Validation
summary.description: Comprehensive validation of multi-tenant isolation guarantees across Data Cloud infrastructure, including entity storage, event logs, query operations, and governance plane policies and rules.

coverageAreas: Entity saved by tenant A is invisible to tenant B
Each tenant sees only its own entities
Delete by tenant A does not affect tenant B's entities
findById across tenants returns empty, not other tenant's entity
Events appended by tenant A are invisible to tenant B
Event queries by type are tenant-scoped
TenantContext metadata does not leak across instances
Same collection name in different tenants are isolated namespaces
Concurrent entity saves maintain tenant isolation
Full CRUD lifecycle maintains tenant boundaries
Full event lifecycle maintains tenant boundaries
Policies defined by tenant A are invisible to tenant B
Each tenant sees only its own governance rules
Audit logs are tenant-scoped in governance plane

hardeningFeatures.description: All entity operations (save, query, findById, delete) are automatically scoped to the tenant context. Cross-tenant access returns empty results, not errors.
hardeningFeatures.implementation: DataCloudClient methods accept tenantId parameter and enforce isolation at the storage layer
hardeningFeatures.testCoverage: EntityCrudIsolation nested test class with 4 tests

hardeningFeatures.description: Event append and query operations are tenant-scoped. Events emitted by one tenant are never visible to other tenants.
hardeningFeatures.implementation: EventStoreContract enforces tenant boundaries at the event log layer
hardeningFeatures.testCoverage: EventLogIsolation nested test class with 2 tests

hardeningFeatures.description: Immutable value object for tenant context with tenant ID, optional workspace ID, and metadata. Factory methods ensure correct scoping and immutability.
hardeningFeatures.implementation: TenantContext record with validation, factory methods, and withMetadata/withWorkspace builders
hardeningFeatures.testCoverage: TenantContextSpi nested test class with 4 tests

hardeningFeatures.description: Same collection names in different tenants are isolated namespaces. Each tenant has its own data namespace regardless of collection naming.
hardeningFeatures.implementation: Storage layer prefixes tenant context to collection namespace
hardeningFeatures.testCoverage: CrossCollectionIsolation nested test class with 1 test

hardeningFeatures.description: Concurrent operations from multiple tenants maintain isolation without cross-contamination under load.
hardeningFeatures.implementation: Eventloop-based async execution with tenant-scoped operations
hardeningFeatures.testCoverage: ConcurrentAccess nested test class with 1 test (20 ops per tenant)

hardeningFeatures.description: Full CRUD and event lifecycles maintain tenant boundaries throughout create, read, update, delete operations.
hardeningFeatures.implementation: End-to-end integration tests verify isolation across operation sequences
hardeningFeatures.testCoverage: EndToEndLifecycle nested test class with 2 tests

areas: Policies defined by tenant A are invisible to tenant B
Each tenant sees only its own governance rules
Audit logs are tenant-scoped in governance plane

services.purpose: API for governing memory namespaces: enforcing retention policies, controlling access to namespaces, and auditing governance decisions
services.tenantScoping: All methods (enforceRetention, evaluateAccess, setRetentionPolicy, auditLog) accept tenantId parameter and enforce tenant boundaries
capabilities: Register and update retention policies per namespace
Evaluate whether a memory access is permitted for a given principal
Expire and evict memory entries past their retention window

services.purpose: Domain contract for data governance policy management
services.tenantScoping: Policy record includes tenantId field. Policies are indexed by tenant and only applied to matching tenant contexts

services.purpose: Evaluates governance policies against data artifacts and surfaces violations
services.tenantScoping: EvaluationContext includes tenantId. evaluateAll method filters policies by tenantId match before evaluation
services.evaluationLogic: Policies with null tenantId are treated as global. Policies with tenantId only apply to matching tenant contexts

hardeningFeatures.description: Policies defined by one tenant are invisible to other tenants. Each tenant sees only its own governance rules.
hardeningFeatures.implementation: Policy record includes tenantId field. Query operations filter by tenant context

hardeningFeatures.description: Policy evaluation is tenant-scoped. A tenant's policies only apply to operations within that tenant's context.
hardeningFeatures.implementation: PolicyEvaluator.evaluateAll filters policies by tenantId match before evaluation

hardeningFeatures.description: Audit logs in the governance plane are tenant-scoped. Each tenant sees only its own audit events.
hardeningFeatures.implementation: Event log queries are filtered by tenant context. Audit events include tenant-specific actor information

hardeningFeatures.description: Memory governance operations (retention enforcement, access evaluation, policy setting, audit logging) are all tenant-scoped.
hardeningFeatures.implementation: MemoryGovernanceService methods require tenantId parameter and enforce boundaries

tenantContextSpi.purpose: Tenant context for multi-tenant storage operations
validation: workspaceId defaults to Optional.empty() if null
metadata is copied to immutable map if provided, defaults to empty map
tenantContextSpi.immutability: Record with defensive copying ensures immutability. withMetadata and withWorkspace return new instances.
tenantContextSpi.testCoverage: TenantContextTest with nested test classes for factory methods, metadata handling, and workspace scoping

testSuites.name: TenantContextTest
testSuites.coverage: Factory methods, metadata handling, workspace scoping, immutability

testSuites.name: MemoryGovernanceServiceTest
testSuites.coverage: Governance service tests, access decision tests, audit log, retention enforcement

testSuites.name: PolicyEvaluatorTest
testSuites.coverage: Policy evaluation for all policy types, tenant-scoped evaluation

isolationGuarantees: No cross-tenant data leakage in entity storage
No cross-tenant event leakage in event logs
Immutable tenant context prevents accidental scope changes
Concurrent operations maintain isolation under load

securityConsiderations: Tenant ID is required and validated (non-null, non-blank)
All storage operations are implicitly tenant-scoped
Cross-tenant access returns empty results, not errors (prevents information leakage through error messages)
Policies are tenant-scoped and only apply to matching tenant contexts
Audit logs are tenant-scoped for compliance and investigation
Metadata is immutable to prevent scope creep
Workspace ID provides additional scoping within tenant
notes: Tenant isolation is enforced at multiple layers: storage, event log, governance plane
All test suites use ActiveJ eventloop for async execution consistent with Data Cloud architecture
Governance plane policies include tenantId field for scoping
PolicyEvaluator filters policies by tenantId before evaluation
Audit logs in governance plane are tenant-scoped for compliance
